cards.title: Asset called Life
cards.content:
ΓÇó Life insurance protects against the financial loss caused by premature death (dying too soon) or living too long (outliving savings).
ΓÇó Human Life Value (HLV): Method to calculate how much insurance one needs based on their future earning capacity.

cards.title: Nomination vs Assignment
cards.content:
ΓÇó Nomination (Sec 39): Appointing someone to receive the claim money if the insured dies. Policy ownership remains with the insured.
ΓÇó Assignment (Sec 38): Transferring the absolute ownership and rights of the policy to another person/entity (e.g., to a bank for a loan).

items.text: 30 Days: Time given to the policyholder to return the policy if they don't like the terms.
Voluntarily terminating the policy before maturity to get the "Surrender Value" (a reduced amount).
Restarting a lapsed policy by paying unpaid premiums along with interest/penalty within the revival period.

sections.title: ≡ƒÆí Exam Master Tips
items: Insurable Interest in Life Insurance must exist ONLY at the time of taking the policy.
An assignment automatically cancels any previous nomination (except when assigned to the insurer for a loan).
Loans can usually be taken against a Life Insurance policy after it acquires a surrender value (typically 2-3 years).
Life insurance is a "Contract of Assurance", meaning the event (death or maturity) is certain to happen.
If a policy document is lost, the insurer issues a Duplicate Policy after obtaining an indemnity bond.
